summary: Machine policy for whether users can delete diagnostic data from Settings. Microsoft documents the same device policy path, value name, default, and numeric meanings that the app writes.

setting.name: Diagnostic Data Deletion
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can offer a Settings action that deletes diagnostic data associated with the device. This policy decides whether that delete option is available.
setting.why_people_change_it: Organizations may disable the delete action to standardize support or data-retention behavior. Privacy-focused personal users often prefer to leave the option available.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling the delete action removes a user-facing control and can simplify management, but it also takes away an obvious privacy control from the person using the device.
setting.professional_notes: DataCollection.admx maps this machine policy to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection\DisableDeviceDelete with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force the diagnostic-data deletion policy.

allowed_values.label: Diagnostic data deletion allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and the delete option remains available.

allowed_values.label: Diagnostic data deletion disabled
allowed_values.meaning: The delete option is blocked by policy.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableDeviceDelete = 1, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows device-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 version 1809 and newer supported systems

states.rationale: The machine policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: This is the policy baseline rather than a guarantee that every UI surface is identical across Windows editions.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who want the ordinary Settings delete option available
avoid_for: Strictly managed environments that want this action blocked
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its standard policy behavior for diagnostic-data deletion.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need to block the delete UI through policy.
tradeoffs: The user-facing delete option can remain available.
No management restriction is enforced for this action.

recommended_profiles.label: Managed retention
intended_for: Environments with a controlled support workflow

states.rationale: Block the diagnostic-data delete action by policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the delete action removed from Settings on managed systems.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the local user to keep an obvious privacy control.
tradeoffs: The Settings delete action is no longer available.
Users lose a direct way to clear diagnostic data from the device UI.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: DisableDeviceDelete
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the policy that controls whether users can delete diagnostic data from Settings.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft DataCollection.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file shows DisableDeviceDelete with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under the DataCollection policy key.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableDeviceDelete = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak provenance record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

evidence.title: nohuto win-config mirror - DisableDeviceDelete policy
evidence.summary: DataCollection.admx shows DisableDeviceDelete under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection. The mirrored ExplainText says enabling the policy disables the Delete diagnostic data button, while disabling or not configuring it leaves the button enabled. EnabledValue=1 and DisabledValue=0 are explicit.

decision.why: Microsoft documents DisableDeviceDelete directly in the System Policy CSP and maps it to Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection\DisableDeviceDelete with 0 as the default not-disabled state and 1 as disabled. The app writes that exact machine policy surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: This policy setting controls whether the Delete diagnostic data button is enabled in Diagnostic & feedback Settings page. Default Value 0; Allowed values: 0 (Default) Not disabled. 1 Disabled. Group policy mapping: Registry Key Name Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection; Registry Value Name DisableDeviceDelete.
validation_proof.notes: The System Policy CSP page explicitly names the exact machine policy path and value name the app writes and defines the 0/1 semantics. Added nohuto mirror corroboration via nohuto-datacollection-delete-admx.
